TIE FAHAOE ! 7AI WAS HEAOY


The parade moved on time, with
ID. G. Coates, marshal of the day,
and bis aid, A. w. Swenson, in the
van. There was no police escort or
other military display at the head
Of the procession, and the grand
marshal marched on foot like the
rest of the men. Bowen's band
headed the parade. with the Orion
band toward the center and Reem-
er's band at the head of the car
penters. The parade was over a
mile and half long and took from
30 to 40 minutes to pass given
point. The general committee for
the day and the trades council fol.
lowed the first band.


In the first division were the pro.
vision and culinary trades, the bak-
ers, bartenders, cigarmakers, COOKS,
walters and Mattresses.


In the second division, the beer
trades section, were the beer driv-
ers, bottlers, brewers and coopers.


In the third. or printing trades
section, were the bookbinders, en-
gravers, mailers, pressmen, press
feeders, printers and stereotypers.


Grouped in the miscellaneous Sec
tion were the Card and Label
